title: Compiler Design
description: Study of compiler construction covering lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate code generation, optimization, and code generation. Students implement a compiler for a simple programming language through a series of progressive assignments. Topics include regular expressions and finite automata, context-free grammars, LL and LR parsing, type checking, intermediate representations, and basic optimization techniques. Both CS301 (for algorithm design) and CS202 (for formal languages and automata theory foundations) are required.
outcomes: Implement a lexical analyzer using regular expressions
Build parsers using LL and LR techniques
Construct abstract syntax trees from source code
Perform type checking and semantic analysis
